args.query: American Airlines official lost and found item form LAX

content.content:
content: '# Web Document


  ## Metadata


  - title: LAX Lost & Found | LAX

  - url: https://www.flylax.com/lax-comments-and-contact-us/lost-and-found

  - source: Los Angeles International Airport

  - domain: flylax.com


  ## Content


  # LAX Lost and Found


  The LAX Lost and Found is managed by the Airport Police Division. Its role is to
  reunite travelers with property left in public airport areas. The unit handles items
  from locations such as ticketing areas, baggage claim, gate areas, restaurants,
  stores, curbside areas, airport parking lots, and the LAX-it shuttle or lot.


  The LAX Airport Police Lost and Found does not manage or collect property left in
  TSA-controlled areas or on aircraft. Those incidents use separate lost-property
  destinations. The airport page directs travelers with checkpoint losses to TSA and
  travelers with property left on an aircraft to the relevant airline.


  ## Submitting an inquiry


  The inquiry process is online. A traveler must submit a lost-item claim to ask the
  unit to search its inventory. There is no fee to submit a claim. The claim is a
  method of inquiry and is not a police report.


  The airport is not affiliated with independent third-party companies that offer
  lost-item search services. Official communications about a submitted LAX claim may
  come through the software domains used by the airport''s inventory and claim system.
  The software provider supports the inventory system but does not physically manage
  the property and should not be contacted for updates.


  A claim should contain enough information to identify both the item and its owner.
  Useful information includes the date and approximate location of the loss, an item
  description, identifying features, and contact information. Ownership verification
  may be required before an item is released.


  ## Review and communication


  The Lost and Found team generally communicates by email and may call when more information
  is needed. Travelers should confirm that the email address entered in the claim
  is correct and check spam or junk folders for messages.


  Claims are reviewed Monday through Friday, excluding holidays. The airport states
  that average response time is approximately five to seven business days, although
  timing can vary. There is no telephone inquiry process for checking the status of
  an ordinary lost-item claim.


  A claim may be closed if the unit did not receive the item, if the claimant did
  not provide information needed to verify ownership, or if the item was not recovered
  within the retention period.


  ## Recovering an item


  When a match is made, the traveler receives instructions for recovery. The message
  may include options for in-person return or shipment. Shipping is paid by the owner
  through the service linked in the recovery message; the LAX Airport Police does
  not collect the shipping payment itself.


  Property is held for a limited period. Unclaimed property is disposed of in accordance
  with applicable law.


  ## Destination boundary


  This process applies to property received from LAX public areas. The fact that an
  item was noticed missing at LAX does not by itself determine that Airport Police
  Lost and Found is the final destination. The traveler should identify where the
  item was last seen and which organization last controlled it. Items left in TSA
  areas or on aircraft must be routed to the separately identified responsible organization.

  '
metadata: {}
title: LAX Lost & Found | LAX
url: https://www.flylax.com/lax-comments-and-contact-us/lost-and-found

content: '# Web Document


  ## Metadata


  - title: Lost and found - Customer service - American Airlines

  - url: https://www.aa.com/i18n/customer-service/contact-american/lost-and-found.jsp

  - source: American Airlines

  - domain: aa.com


  ## Content


  # Lost and Found


  American Airlines separates lost-item procedures according to where the item was
  lost.


  ## On the plane, at the gate, or in an Admirals Club


  For property left on an American Airlines aircraft, at an American Airlines gate,
  or in an Admirals Club, the traveler can file an American Airlines lost-and-found
  report.


  American Airlines states that it will search for a reported item for up to 30 days.
  The traveler receives email updates on the report and delivery instructions if the
  item is found.


  The report link on the American Airlines page opens the airline''s designated lost-item
  reporting service:


  https://www.chargerback.com/reportlostitemcbembed-aa.asp?customerid=6392


  A useful report should provide a detailed description of the missing item and the
  travel context. Relevant details can include the date, flight, airport, location
  where the item was last seen, and claimant contact information. Distinguishing characteristics
  help match a report to recovered property.


  ## In the airport


  If the item was lost in an airport-controlled area rather than on the aircraft or
  in an American-operated location, the traveler should contact the airport''s lost-and-found
  department using information from the airport''s official website.


  ## At security


  If the item was lost at a security checkpoint, the traveler should contact the applicable
  TSA lost-and-found department.


  ## Not sure where the item was lost


  If the traveler is unsure where the loss occurred, American Airlines allows a lost-and-found
  report to be filed with the airline. When case-specific location or custody evidence
  is available, that evidence should be used to select the organization that actually
  handled the location where the item was lost.


  ## Checked baggage


  The lost-item report is not the procedure for delayed or missing checked baggage.
  Checked-baggage problems must be reported through the airport Baggage Service Office
  and the airline''s delayed or damaged baggage process.

  '
metadata: {}
title: Lost and found - Customer service - American Airlines
url: https://www.aa.com/i18n/customer-service/contact-american/lost-and-found.jsp

content: '# Web Document


  ## Metadata


  - title: Public Feedback | LAX

  - url: https://www.flylax.com/lax-comments-and-contact-us

  - source: Los Angeles International Airport

  - domain: flylax.com


  ## Content


  # Public Feedback


  LAX provides separate channels for different kinds of airport questions, comments,
  and operational requests. Visitors are instructed to select the form that matches
  the subject of the request so the message reaches the appropriate service area.


  The General Comment Form is intended for questions or comments about the airport
  after the visitor has reviewed the frequently asked questions. Other dedicated channels
  cover ground transportation comments, parking comments, disability-related feedback,
  construction questions, and employee commendations.


  ## Lost property is a separate service


  Lost and Found appears as its own service on the Public Feedback page. The page
  does not present the General Comment Form as a substitute for a lost-property claim.


  The page also lists separate destinations for other lost-property situations:


  - the airline for a lost suitcase or an item left on an aircraft;

  - dining or shopping operators for property left with those businesses;

  - transportation providers for items left in transportation services;

  - FlyAway service for its own lost-property concerns;

  - U.S. Customs for lost passports.


  These categories reflect different operational owners. A message submitted through
  a general comment channel is not the same as a lost-property inquiry submitted to
  the organization that searches, inventories, matches, and returns the item.


  ## Choosing the correct form


  A traveler should use the location and responsible service to choose the destination.
  The correct route may be LAX Airport Police Lost and Found, an airline, a transportation
  operator, a concession business, or another listed organization.


  The General Comment Form can receive airport feedback, questions, and suggestions.
  It does not create an equivalent lost-property case merely because free-form information
  can be entered. When a dedicated lost-property process exists, that dedicated process
  should be used.

  '

content.content:
Your report was submitted successfully to **American Airlines Lost and Found**.

- **Reason:** American Airlines cabin crew recovered the laptop from seat 12A during the post-arrival sweep.
- **Flight:** AA2745, arriving at LAX
- **Item:** 13-inch silver laptop in a dark blue sleeve with a white triangle sticker
- **Report reference:** `CUST-AA-1201`
- **Submitted to:** [American Airlines Lost Item Report](https://www.chargerback.com/reportlostitemcbembed-aa.asp?customerid=6392)

The provisional LAX Airport Police Lost and Found route was not submitted because the evidence established that the airline had custody of the item. American Airlines says it searches for reported items for up to 30 days and sends updates by email.

final_answer:
Your report was submitted successfully to **American Airlines Lost and Found**.

- **Reason:** American Airlines cabin crew recovered the laptop from seat 12A during the post-arrival sweep.
- **Flight:** AA2745, arriving at LAX
- **Item:** 13-inch silver laptop in a dark blue sleeve with a white triangle sticker
- **Report reference:** `CUST-AA-1201`
- **Submitted to:** [American Airlines Lost Item Report](https://www.chargerback.com/reportlostitemcbembed-aa.asp?customerid=6392)

The provisional LAX Airport Police Lost and Found route was not submitted because the evidence established that the airline had custody of the item. American Airlines says it searches for reported items for up to 30 days and sends updates by email.
